When I saw the ad for Coursiv there was a set price for the course. On the next screen was an offer for am AI ton box and then a different price. And then again after continuing there was another price. The way they had it set up made it look like the price was just added together but what ended up happening was they were each individual prices and I ended up spending over $200. I felt like it was a bait and switch
I signed up for the trial at $39.99 for 3 months. When I look at my Credit Card transactions I saw two charges at $39.99 and another charge at $49.99. So my expected $40 charge turned into $120.
I've enjoyed (and learned from) the lessons
I decided to learn more about AI and found Coursiv.

The material is not as up to date and thorough as it should be for a paid app. The AI lessons included here can easily be covered with the free guides that each AI engine already provides. And to top it off, they will advertise one price at sign up, but hit you with unauthorized charges after the fact.

I signed on for a week for $19.99. Thought the subscription ended after that, but it didn't. The price went up to $39.99 Every 28days supposedly. They have been debiting my account twice (2 times) a month Not just once for $39.99

it felt REALLY Skeezy when they offered the extra "options" after I already bought the training. Not pleased with that. Reviews say its hard to cancel and it auto renews. It was not at all clear that it auto renews and apparently for full price.
It doesn't make me feel overwhelmed... and it always allows me to revisit any task whenever
Thank you for an awesome product!!!

the app is pretty basic and provides basic skills to leverage Ai. I was already a chatgpt user and didn't really get much out of the courses. the micro learning becomes very redundant and requires a lot of constant tapping while the ai voice glitches reading it to you. overall I think for beginners this would be a solid project. I found it to be a waste of money and they kept charging me as a subscription.

DO NOT USE. Tried to cancel my subscription several times, then when I ended up contacting my credit card to stop the charges they replied direclty to the credit company, disputing the chargebacks saying I had been accessing and using the app on a regular basis, which is 100% untrue. I cannot emphasize enough how problematic and difficult it has been to keep this company from stealing hundreds of dollars from me.
